text.title: spatial-interaction-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns repeatedly observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Grab works only with two-hand gesture; one-handed users locked out; store review fails.
antipattern.fix: Declare fallback in spec. Validate with auditor before submission.

antipattern.symptom: User starts drag, hand leaves tracking volume; UI freezes in dragging state until app restart.
antipattern.root-cause: State machine omitted cancel + timeout transitions.
antipattern.fix: Add cancel state triggered by tracking loss + timeout. Add error state for invalid drop targets.

antipattern.symptom: Same app: pinch=select on visionOS, pinch=cancel on Quest; users porting between devices get confused.
antipattern.root-cause: Per-platform implementers chose without referencing shared spec.
antipattern.fix: Spec MUST declare cross-platform parity. Divergence requires explicit justification.

antipattern.symptom: Demo mentions 'voice support'; release ships voice only for 'show settings' command.
antipattern.root-cause: Voice treated as nice-to-have, not full modality.
antipattern.fix: Treat voice as a full modality in the spec; every critical interaction must have a voice intent.
